topic: A person learns their college is closing down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The official notice, stark white against the faded beige of the bulletin board, felt like a physical blow. Liam traced the embossed lettering with a numb finger, the edges of the paper sharp against his skin. Every word swam before his eyes: "Campus operations will cease by the end of the semester." He stood there by the library entrance, the usual hum of student life replaced by an unnerving silence. A cold seeped into his bones, making his hands clammy. He wanted to turn around, to bolt, but his feet seemed rooted to the spot, heavy and unresponsive. He swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the action difficult. He looked up at the imposing brick facade of the administration building, and the shadows seemed to deepen around the edges of his vision.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this?" Maya whispered, her voice thin and reedy, as she stared at the final liquidation schedule tacked to the campus notice board. The numbers swam—dormitory assignments, library book inventories, the very structure of her routine dissolving into meaningless figures. A tremor started in her hands, a slight vibration that she fought to suppress by clenching her fists until her nails bit into her palms. She took a shaky breath, trying to anchor herself to the solid ground beneath her worn sneakers. The air in the hallway suddenly felt too thin, too brittle. She glanced toward the exit, a desperate urge to flee warring with a paralyzing inertia that kept her fixed to the spot.

[story 3]

He kept re-reading the email, the screen illuminating his face with a harsh, unforgiving light. Each sentence repeated itself in a loop, a cruel, monotonous drumbeat in the quiet apartment. A tightness constricted his chest, making each inhale shallow and restricted. He paced the length of his living room three times, the carpet fibers rough beneath his bare feet. When he finally stopped, leaning against the window frame, he watched the setting sun bleed orange across the horizon. The world outside seemed too vast, too indifferent to the impending collapse of everything he had built here. He squeezed his eyes shut, a desperate attempt to shut out the impending reality.
